But is it worth the download and sub? Learn here.","articleBody":"Cleanup App - Phone Cleaner is a multi-featured cleaning application for the iPhone. It comes with a unique set of tools to help you clean up your device\u2019s library efficiently, along with other types of data.    However, the app is definitely not for everyone. For some users, its negative aspects may outweigh its benefits, making it not worth the price. Well, there's only one way to find out! Keep reading for a detailed breakdown of Cleanup App - Phone Cleaner.  Summary  Cleanup App - Phone Cleaner is an iPhone application that can help you clean up your photos, videos, contacts, and email. It was created by a Florida-based developer called DevMeApp, who has one other entry on the App Store called FAX from iPhone.    This review details our experience with Cleanup App; it has a library cleaning kit that we got very excited about, a good interface with decent filter and sorting options, and a well-rounded scope of abilities. However, it has some performance flaws that can (in our opinion) break the experience for some users. We\u2019ll go into much more detail about the app\u2019s strengths and weaknesses in the following sections.  How Much Does Cleanup App - Phone Cleaner Cost?  Cleanup App - Phone Cleaner is on the expensive side and somewhat confusing. Here\u2019s roughly what you can expect:     \t$4.99 per week   \t$7.99 ~ $9.99 per month   \t$39.99 ~ $49.99 per year    But when you opt into Cleanup App\u2019s free trial (lasts 3 days), the default suggested plan costs almost $85. It consists of a 1-year subscription, as well as payment for an add-on called \u201cProtector for iPhone.\u201d The app, unfortunately, doesn\u2019t spell out exactly what that is. Cleanup App\u2019s reviews also note that its usability is very limited without opting into the free trial, so pretty much all users are faced with this confusion.    That said, Cleanup App\u2019s library cleaning features (which we\u2019ll talk about next) can make it worth the price of a subscription. Just make sure that you review its subscription options carefully before paying.    Not keen on paying for a cleanup app? Check out Clever Cleaner: AI Cleanup App on the App Store. It\u2019s completely free\u2014no trials, subscriptions, or limitations. It\u2019s a powerful video and photo clean-up app for your library (with tools like similar photo detection, video compression, Live photo compression, etc.), which usually takes up the most storage space on your device.  Main Features  In this section, we\u2019ll give you a brief but detailed outline of what the iPhone\u2019s Cleanup App - Phone Cleaner has to offer in terms of features. We love that it has almost no bloat, while presenting a very useful core set of features. At the same time, it offers unique tools we don\u2019t see in other cleaning applications. Here\u2019s a detailed list of everything this app has to offer:     \tLibrary clutter cleaner: Eliminate duplicate photos, similar images, and screenshots from your library. Easily find your clutter using a date range filter and sorting options according to size and recency.   \tSmart Clean: Purge all the clutter in your library with one button. If you accidentally get rid of photos or videos you still need, you can restore them via the Recently Deleted folder in the Photos app.   \tSelfie management: Clean up unwanted selfies and only keep the best one, automatically selected for you by the app. It\u2019s part of the library cleaner menu, but this gets its own entry because we don\u2019t see this feature often.   \tVideo cleaner & compressor: Delete similar videos and keep the best one. If you don\u2019t want to delete anything, you can compress videos instead; you get several compression options, depending on the quality of the original photo.   \tEmail cleaner: See all of your emails according to the sender and delete all emails from each one. The app also has a feature that lets you unsubscribe from senders right then.   \tContacts manager: Find and remove incomplete contacts cluttering up your address book. A duplicate contacts cleaner feature exists, but it didn\u2019t seem to find our duplicate contacts.   \tSwipe mode: Review individual photos efficiently using gestures. Swipe left to delete a photo or video, or swipe right to keep it.   \tKeep List: Review all the photos and videos you chose specifically to keep in one universal page (found in the Settings menu).   \tWidgets: Add unique widgets to your home screen that show your device\u2019s available storage space and current battery level.    How to Use Cleanup App - Phone Cleaner  It\u2019s impossible to answer the question \u201cis Cleanup a good app?\u201d with a few lists and paragraphs. To give you an accurate and detailed review of Cleanup App, we prepared a full demonstration of our experience using it to clean up our own iPhone.    Below is a set of instructions based exactly on what we did; you can follow along if you started the free trial, or you can simply check out how the app works without having to download it yourself.    Step 1. Open the App Store and download Cleanup App - Phone Cleaner. Once you launch it for the first time, you\u2019ll be asked for access to your library\u2014grant Full Access to maximize its features.    Step 2. We started with the Cleanup tool (selectable in the bottom menu), which presented us with several categories of clutter. Similar photos were a great way to start\u2014we found many identical, non-exact duplicate photos (exact duplicates are in their own category). You can tap the Smart Clean button to get rid of all junk files immediately, but we\u2019ll show you a more hands-on experience.    Step 3. After clearing up similar photos, duplicate photos, and obsolete screenshots, the Swipe tool came next. It allows you to quickly review and delete photos using swipe gestures for a more precise organizing effort.        Step 4. Back in the Cleanup tool, videos are next. Like similar photos, similar videos have their own category, allowing you to choose the best and get rid of the rest.    Step 5. The video compressor is accessible via the All Videos menu, and it helps shrink video files without severely impacting quality. We were super impressed with the sheer number of options available! Browsing videos was also made much easier by the filter and sorting options.        Step 6. After tidying up media files, we tried organizing our contacts. Unfortunately, only the Incomplete Contacts tool was of any use. The app couldn\u2019t find any of our duplicate contacts.    Step 7. The email cleaner was much more impressive; it allowed us to instantly purge hundreds of emails from specific senders, while giving us the option to unsubscribe in-app.    Step 8. Finally, we opened the Keep List in the app\u2019s Settings menu to review our saved media. Being able to double-check our selection was a nice touch.        All deleted items are still saved in the Recently Deleted folder in the native Photos app. Make sure to delete them again from there to free up storage space.  Pros & Cons  All iPhone cleaner applications have their own unique strengths and weaknesses, and Cleanup App - Phone Cleaner is no exception. In terms of strengths, Cleanup App\u2019s feature offering easily takes the spotlight.    However, we ran into some performance issues that you should be aware of before downloading. See below for more details.       \tThe app has a good collection of features that are all relevant to cleanup   \tIts interface is clean; the app does a good job of organizing its features and buttons   \tThe selfie management feature is relatively unique, and it\u2019s effective in its execution   \tIts similar photo detection algorithm is really good compared to many similar apps         \tWe experienced interface lag when loading pages and reviewing individual photos or videos; we find this pretty egregious for a paid app   \tThe contacts cleaner didn\u2019t detect our manually created duplicate contacts   \tConfusing and expensive pricing; the default option is a 1-year subscription plus an add-on that totals to almost $85      Is Cleanup App - Phone Cleaner Safe for iPhones?  Yes, Cleanup App - Phone Cleaner is safe to use. It did not cause data loss on our device, and it didn\u2019t modify any settings that would cause issues with synchronization (beyond granting access to the library). But with any third-party application that handles your data, we recommend creating a backup before using it to clean up your device.    Most importantly, the app is completely free of malware, making it safe to install.  Final Assessment  Cleanup App - Phone Cleaner impressed and disappointed us in the same breath. We were genuinely keen on the kit it came with; namely, its similar detection tool and selfie management feature are relatively uncommon, and they perform their tasks well.    The Keep List is another standout feature we would like to see in other applications. It's convenient to have the ability to review all the media items you saved to make sure \"it's all there.\" And overall, the app is well-rounded and thoughtfully designed. All of its tools are easy to find and use, and multiple filtering and sorting options are available in all of the menus.    However, we were very put off by the performance issues we experienced while using the app. It doesn't really get better the more you use it, so you'll just have to live with it. We expect a much more polished user experience for paid apps, especially considering its price.    Overall, Cleanup App - Phone Cleaner could be a very strong contender for one of the best iPhone cleaners on the App Store--but only if the developers fix the lag issue and improve the contacts cleaner.","keywords":"","datePublished":"2025-11-19T13:53:34+00:00","dateModified":"2025-11-19T12:51:41+00:00","author":[{"@type":"Person","name":"Alejandro Santos","description":"Alejandro is Macgasm\u2019s Chief Writer and Apple ecosystem enthusiast. Assessment">Final Assessment</a></li></ul></nav></div><h2><span class="ez-toc-section" id="Summary"></span>Summary<span class="ez-toc-section-end"></span></h2><p><a href="https://phonecleaner.app/" target="_blank" rel="noopener nofollow" data-wpel-link="external">Cleanup App – Phone Cleaner</a> is an iPhone application that can help you clean up your photos, videos, contacts, and email. It was created by a Florida-based developer called DevMeApp, who has one other entry on the App Store called <a href="https://apps.apple.com/us/app/fax-from-iphone-fax-app/id1563922708" target="_blank" rel="noopener nofollow" data-wpel-link="external">FAX from iPhone</a>.</p><p>This review details our experience with Cleanup App; it has a library cleaning kit that we got very excited about, a good interface with decent filter and sorting options, and a well-rounded scope of abilities. However, it has some performance flaws that can (in our opinion) break the experience for some users. We’ll go into much more detail about the app’s strengths and weaknesses in the following sections.</p><h2><span class="ez-toc-section" id="How_Much_Does_Cleanup_App_-_Phone_Cleaner_Cost"></span>How Much Does Cleanup App – Phone Cleaner Cost?<span class="ez-toc-section-end"></span></h2><p>Cleanup App – Phone Cleaner is on the expensive side and somewhat confusing. Here’s roughly what you can expect:</p><ul><li>$4.99 per week</li><li>$7.99 ~ $9.99 per month</li><li>$39.99 ~ $49.99 per year</li></ul><p>But when you opt into Cleanup App’s free trial (lasts 3 days), the default suggested plan costs almost $85. It consists of a 1-year subscription, as well as payment for an add-on called “Protector for iPhone.” The app, unfortunately, doesn’t spell out exactly what that is. Cleanup App’s reviews also note that its usability is very limited without opting into the free trial, so pretty much all users are faced with this confusion.</p><p>That said, Cleanup App’s library cleaning features (which we’ll talk about next) can make it worth the price of a subscription. Just make sure that you review its subscription options carefully before paying.</p><div class="sc_note_orange-full sc_color_border-orange sc_color_border">Not keen on paying for a cleanup app? Check out <a href="https://apps.apple.com/us/app/clever-cleaner-ai-cleanup-app/id1666645584" target="_blank" rel="noopener follow" data-wpel-link="exclude">Clever Cleaner: AI Cleanup App</a> on the App Store. It’s completely free—no trials, subscriptions, or limitations. It’s a powerful video and photo clean-up app for your library (with tools like similar photo detection, video compression, Live photo compression, etc.), which usually takes up the most storage space on your device.</div><h2><span class="ez-toc-section" id="Main_Features"></span>Main Features<span class="ez-toc-section-end"></span></h2><p>In this section, we’ll give you a brief but detailed outline of what the iPhone’s Cleanup App – Phone Cleaner has to offer in terms of features. We love that it has almost no bloat, while presenting a very useful core set of features. At the same time, it offers unique tools we don’t see in other cleaning applications. Here’s a detailed list of everything this app has to offer:</p><ul><li><span class="sc_badge sc_badge_border-black">Library clutter cleaner:</span> Eliminate duplicate photos, similar images, and screenshots from your library. Easily find your clutter using a date range filter and sorting options according to size and recency.</li><li><span class="sc_badge sc_badge_border-black">Smart Clean:</span> Purge all the clutter in your library with one button. If you accidentally get rid of photos or videos you still need, you can restore them via the <span class="text-strong">Recently Deleted</span> folder in the Photos app.</li><li><span class="sc_badge sc_badge_border-black">Selfie management:</span> Clean up unwanted selfies and only keep the best one, automatically selected for you by the app. It’s part of the library cleaner menu, but this gets its own entry because we don’t see this feature often.</li><li><span class="sc_badge sc_badge_border-black">Video cleaner & compressor:</span> Delete similar videos and keep the best one. If you don’t want to delete anything, you can compress videos instead; you get several compression options, depending on the quality of the original photo.</li><li><span class="sc_badge sc_badge_border-black">Email cleaner:</span> See all of your emails according to the sender and delete all emails from each one. The app also has a feature that lets you unsubscribe from senders right then.</li><li><span class="sc_badge sc_badge_border-black">Contacts manager:</span> Find and remove incomplete contacts cluttering up your address book. A duplicate contacts cleaner feature exists, but it didn’t seem to find our duplicate contacts.</li><li><span class="sc_badge sc_badge_border-black">Swipe mode:</span> Review individual photos efficiently using gestures. Swipe left to delete a photo or video, or swipe right to keep it.</li><li><span class="sc_badge sc_badge_border-black">Keep List:</span> Review all the photos and videos you chose specifically to keep in one universal page (found in the Settings menu).</li><li><span class="sc_badge sc_badge_border-black">Widgets:</span> Add unique widgets to your home screen that show your device’s available storage space and current battery level.</li></ul><h2><span class="ez-toc-section" id="How_to_Use_Cleanup_App_-_Phone_Cleaner"></span>How to Use Cleanup App – Phone Cleaner<span class="ez-toc-section-end"></span></h2><p>It’s impossible to answer the question “is Cleanup a good app?” with a few lists and paragraphs. To give you an accurate and detailed review of Cleanup App, we prepared a full demonstration of our experience using it to clean up our own iPhone.</p><p>Below is a set of instructions based exactly on what we did; you can follow along if you started the free trial, or you can simply check out how the app works without having to download it yourself.</p><p><span class="sc_badge sc_badge_border-black">Step 1.</span> Open the App Store and <a href="https://apps.apple.com/us/app/cleanup-app-phone-cleaner/id1541639300" target="_blank" rel="noopener nofollow" data-wpel-link="external">download Cleanup App – Phone Cleaner</a>. Once you launch it for the first time, you’ll be asked for access to your library—grant <span class="text-strong">Full Access</span> to maximize its features.</p><p><span class="sc_badge sc_badge_border-black">Step 2.</span> We started with the <span class="text-strong">Cleanup</span> tool (selectable in the bottom menu), which presented us with several categories of clutter. Similar photos were a great way to start—we found many identical, non-exact duplicate photos (exact duplicates are in their own category). You can tap the <span class="text-strong">Smart Clean</span> button to get rid of all junk files immediately, but we’ll show you a more hands-on experience.</p><p><span class="sc_badge sc_badge_border-black">Step 3.</span> After clearing up similar photos, duplicate photos, and obsolete screenshots, the <span class="text-strong">Swipe</span> tool came next. It allows you to quickly review and delete photos using swipe gestures for a more precise organizing effort.</p><p><img decoding="async" fetchpriority="high" class="lazyload aligncenter size-full wp-image-207930" src="/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/Cleanup-App-Photo-Tools.jpg" data-srcset="https://news.macgasm.net/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/Cleanup-App-Photo-Tools.jpg 1333w, https://news.macgasm.net/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/Cleanup-App-Photo-Tools-300x203.jpg 300w, https://news.macgasm.net/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/Cleanup-App-Photo-Tools-500x338.jpg 500w, https://news.macgasm.net/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/Cleanup-App-Photo-Tools-768x519.jpg 768w, https://news.macgasm.net/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/Cleanup-App-Photo-Tools-696x470.jpg 696w, https://news.macgasm.net/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/Cleanup-App-Photo-Tools-1068x721.jpg 1068w" data-lazy-type="image" data-original="/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/Cleanup-App-Photo-Tools.jpg" alt="Cleanup App - Phone Cleaner's photo cleaning tools" width="1333" height="900" srcset="data:image/png;base64," data-sizes="(max-width: 1333px) 100vw, 1333px"></p><p><span class="sc_badge sc_badge_border-black">Step 4.</span> Back in the <span class="text-strong">Cleanup</span> tool, videos are next. Like similar photos, similar videos have their own category, allowing you to choose the best and get rid of the rest.</p><p><span class="sc_badge sc_badge_border-black">Step 5.</span> The video compressor is accessible via the <span class="text-strong">All Videos</span> menu, and it helps shrink video files without severely impacting quality. We were super impressed with the sheer number of options available! Browsing videos was also made much easier by the filter and sorting options.</p><p><img decoding="async" class="lazyload aligncenter size-full wp-image-207931" src="/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/Cleanup-App-Video-Tools.jpg" data-srcset="https://news.macgasm.net/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/Cleanup-App-Video-Tools.jpg 1333w, https://news.macgasm.net/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/Cleanup-App-Video-Tools-300x203.jpg 300w, https://news.macgasm.net/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/Cleanup-App-Video-Tools-500x338.jpg 500w, https://news.macgasm.net/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/Cleanup-App-Video-Tools-768x519.jpg 768w, https://news.macgasm.net/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/Cleanup-App-Video-Tools-696x470.jpg 696w, https://news.macgasm.net/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/Cleanup-App-Video-Tools-1068x721.jpg 1068w" data-lazy-type="image" data-original="/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/Cleanup-App-Video-Tools.jpg" alt="Cleanup App - Phone Cleaner's video cleaning tools" width="1333" height="900" srcset="data:image/png;base64," data-sizes="(max-width: 1333px) 100vw, 1333px"></p><p><span class="sc_badge sc_badge_border-black">Step 6.</span> After tidying up media files, we tried organizing our contacts. Unfortunately, only the <span class="text-strong">Incomplete Contacts</span> tool was of any use. The app couldn’t find any of our duplicate contacts.</p><p><span class="sc_badge sc_badge_border-black">Step 7.</span> The email cleaner was much more impressive; it allowed us to instantly purge hundreds of emails from specific senders, while giving us the option to unsubscribe in-app.</p><p><span class="sc_badge sc_badge_border-black">Step 8.</span> Finally, we opened the <span class="text-strong">Keep List</span> in the app’s <span class="text-strong">Settings</span> menu to review our saved media. Being able to double-check our selection was a nice touch.</p><p><img decoding="async" class="lazyload aligncenter size-full wp-image-207932" src="/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/Cleanup-App-Email-Contacts-Keep.jpg" data-srcset="https://news.macgasm.net/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/Cleanup-App-Email-Contacts-Keep.jpg 1333w, https://news.macgasm.net/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/Cleanup-App-Email-Contacts-Keep-300x203.jpg 300w, https://news.macgasm.net/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/Cleanup-App-Email-Contacts-Keep-500x338.jpg 500w, https://news.macgasm.net/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/Cleanup-App-Email-Contacts-Keep-768x519.jpg 768w, https://news.macgasm.net/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/Cleanup-App-Email-Contacts-Keep-696x470.jpg 696w, https://news.macgasm.net/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/Cleanup-App-Email-Contacts-Keep-1068x721.jpg 1068w" data-lazy-type="image" data-original="/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/Cleanup-App-Email-Contacts-Keep.jpg" alt="Cleanup App - Phone Cleaner's email cleaner, contacts cleaner, and Keep List" width="1333" height="900" srcset="data:image/png;base64," data-sizes="(max-width: 1333px) 100vw, 1333px"></p><div class="sc_note sc_color_border_left sc_color_border_left-black">All deleted items are still saved in the <span class="text-strong">Recently Deleted</span> folder in the native Photos app. Make sure to delete them again from there to free up storage space.</div><h2><span class="ez-toc-section" id="Pros_Cons"></span>Pros & Cons<span class="ez-toc-section-end"></span></h2><p>All iPhone cleaner applications have their own unique strengths and weaknesses, and Cleanup App – Phone Cleaner is no exception. In terms of strengths, Cleanup App’s feature offering easily takes the spotlight.</p><p>However, we ran into some performance issues that you should be aware of before downloading. See below for more details.</p><div class="sc_pros-cons"><div class="pros_cons_list pros_list"><div class="sc_pros-cons-title">Pros:</div><div class="sc_pros-cons-text"><ul><li>The app has a good collection of features that are all relevant to cleanup</li><li>Its interface is clean; the app does a good job of organizing its features and buttons</li><li>The selfie management feature is relatively unique, and it’s effective in its execution</li><li>Its similar photo detection algorithm is really good compared to many similar apps</li></ul></div></div><div class="pros_cons_list cons_list"><div class="sc_pros-cons-title">Cons:</div><div class="sc_pros-cons-text"><ul><li>We experienced interface lag when loading pages and reviewing individual photos or videos; we find this pretty egregious for a paid app</li><li>The contacts cleaner didn’t detect our manually created duplicate contacts</li><li>Confusing and expensive pricing; the default option is a 1-year subscription plus an add-on that totals to almost $85</li></ul></div></div></div><h2><span class="ez-toc-section" id="Is_Cleanup_App_-_Phone_Cleaner_Safe_for_iPhones"></span>Is Cleanup App – Phone Cleaner Safe for iPhones?<span class="ez-toc-section-end"></span></h2><p>Yes, Cleanup App – Phone Cleaner is safe to use. It did not cause data loss on our device, and it didn’t modify any settings that would cause issues with synchronization (beyond granting access to the library). But with any third-party application that handles your data, we recommend creating a backup before using it to clean up your device.</p><p>Most importantly, the app is completely free of malware, making it safe to install.</p><h2><span class="ez-toc-section" id="Final_Assessment"></span>Final Assessment<span class="ez-toc-section-end"></span></h2><p>Cleanup App – Phone Cleaner impressed and disappointed us in the same breath. We were genuinely keen on the kit it came with; namely, its similar detection tool and selfie management feature are relatively uncommon, and they perform their tasks well.</p><p>The Keep List is another standout feature we would like to see in other applications. It’s convenient to have the ability to review all the media items you saved to make sure “it’s all there.” And overall, the app is well-rounded and thoughtfully designed. All of its tools are easy to find and use, and multiple filtering and sorting options are available in all of the menus.</p><p>However, we were very put off by the performance issues we experienced while using the app. It doesn’t really get better the more you use it, so you’ll just have to live with it. We expect a much more polished user experience for paid apps, especially considering its price.</p><p>Overall, Cleanup App – Phone Cleaner could be a very strong contender for one of the best iPhone cleaners on the App Store–but only if the developers fix the lag issue and improve the contacts cleaner.</p></div><div class="td-post-source-tags"></div><div class="td-post-sharing-bottom"><div
